articles.headline: Betting buzz: Manning no longer favorite to go No. 1 in 2026 NFL Draft
articles.description: Arch Manning's odds to be the No. 1 pick in the 2026 NFL Draft drastically shifted after Archie Manning said his grandson would stay at Texas.

images.name: Arch Manning [1296x729]
images.caption: Arch Manning now has the fifth-shortest odds to be the No. 1 pick in the 2026 NFL draft after his grandfather's comments on Thursday.

images.name: Jacob Misiorowski [1296x729]
images.caption: Jacob Misiorowski's odds to win NL Rookie of the Year have fallen from -220 to +900.

images.name: Dak Prescott of Dallas Cowboys [608x342]
images.caption: The Cowboys won seven games last year, and sportsbooks aren't too keen on them doing much better this season.

images.name: Should Arch Manning stay at Texas beyond this season?
images.caption: The "Get Up" crew breaks down Arch Manning's path to the NFL and debates whether he should stay at Texas after this season.

articles.headline: Boise State, Colorado State, Utah State sue Mountain West
articles.description: Boise State, Colorado State and Utah State filed an updated lawsuit arguing the Mountain West and commissioner Gloria Nevarez willfully disregarded the league's bylaws by "intentionally and fraudulently" depriving the schools of their membership rights.

articles.headline: 'Healthy, vibrant' Sanders expects Colorado to 'win differently'
articles.description: After a recent cancer scare, Colorado coach Deion Sanders says he's feeling "healthy and vibrant" ahead of his team's season opener against Georgia Tech on Aug. 29 in Boulder.

images.name: Deion Sanders announces bladder cancer battle
images.caption: Colorado coach Deion Sanders and his medical crew reveal that he battled and beat bladder cancer.

articles.headline: Where is 'College GameDay'? 2025 schedule, locations, recaps
articles.description: It's time for a new season of "College GameDay" on ESPN! Check out the event hub page for weekly site details, recaps and other information.

articles.headline: LSU QB Garrett Nussmeier dealing with patellar tendinitis
articles.description: LSU quarterback Garrett Nussmeier is dealing with patellar tendinitis in his knee after aggravating the issue in practice this week, according to coach Brian Kelly.

images.name: Garrett Nussmeier of the LSU Tigers warms up before a game against the Baylor Bears in the Kinder's Texas Bowl at NRG Stadium on December 31, 2024 in Houston, Texas. [600x400]
images.alt: Garrett Nussmeier of the LSU Tigers warms up before a game against the Baylor Bears in the Kinder's Texas Bowl at NRG Stadium on December 31, 2024 in Houston, Texas.

images.name: Kelly: QB Nussmeier's run game will be crucial to LSU's success
images.caption: The SEC Now crew delves into Garrett Nussmeier's potential on the run and the Tigers' new approach, specifically to their high-stakes opener against Clemson.

articles.headline: Finebaum: John Mateer the reason Oklahoma is ready for big season
articles.description: Paul Finebaum explains why John Mateer is poised to lead Oklahoma's breakout season and shine in the SEC.

article.headline: Taylor, Ingold help No. 4 Wisconsin run over Indiana 45-17
article.description: Jonathan Taylor rushed for 183 yards and one touchdown and Alec Ingold had three scores Saturday to help No. 4 Wisconsin pull away with a 45-17 victory at Indiana.
article.linkText: Taylor, Ingold help No. 4 Wisconsin run over Indiana 45-17

images.name: Wisconsin rolling high as lone unbeaten in Big Ten
images.caption: Wisconsin uses a balanced attack of offense and defense to pick up their 9th win of the season.

video.headline: Wisconsin rolling high as lone unbeaten in Big Ten
video.caption: Wisconsin uses a balanced attack of offense and defense to pick up their 9th win of the season.
video.title: Wisconsin rolling high as lone unbeaten in Big Ten
video.description: Wisconsin uses a balanced attack of offense and defense to pick up their 9th win of the season.

tracking.trackingName: COM_NCF Feature (Wisconsin rolling high as lone unbeaten in Big Ten) 2017/11/04

article.story: BLOOMINGTON, Ind. -- Jonathan Taylor set up No. 4 Wisconsin with a flurry of inside jabs Saturday. Alec Ingold delivered the scoring punches. It was a devastating combination. Taylor ran for 183 yards and one score and Ingold scored three touchdowns on five touches to lead the Badgers to a 45-17 victory at Indiana, keeping them in the shrinking group of unbeaten teams. "We wanted to come out and set the tone," said Taylor, who had a 45-yard run on his first carry after being listed as questionable all week with a bad ankle. "That was the main idea, just coming out and setting the tone." It wasn't as easy or as impressive as the final score indicated. All that really mattered, though, was Wisconsin (9-0, 6-0 Big Ten, No. 9 CFP) stayed in the playoff hunt by extending the nation's second-longest winning streak to 10 and its school record of consecutive victories over conference foes to 12. This victory gives the Badgers an opportunity to take an even bigger step next weekend. Beat Iowa at home and they'll clinch the West Division title and their fifth trip in seven years to the Big Ten Championship game. Perhaps that will show the selection committee Wisconsin is a legitimate contender. For now, beating the Hoosiers (3-6, 0-6) with their traditional script will have to suffice. Just 16 minutes into the game, the Badgers trailed 10-0. But with Taylor gashing the Indiana defense, Bradrick Shaw and Garrett Groshek filling in and Ingold doing the dirty work, Indiana wore down and eventually broke down. Wisconsin took the lead by scoring 24 consecutive points and finally seized control when Taylor, the Big Ten leader in yards rushing and TD runs, broke free for a 32-yard touchdown. "He's just really good," Badgers quarterback Alex Hornibrook said. Indiana cut the deficit to 24-17 with 4:12 left in the third quarter, but then the Badgers' defense started throwing its weight around. Joe Ferguson picked off passes on each of Indiana's next two possessions, and Wisconsin took advantage of the short field with 1-yard scoring runs from Ingold both times to make it 38-17. "We played a great football team today, but when you're minus in the takeaway ratio you're not going to win," Indiana coach Tom Allen said. "To me, that's what it came down to." THE TAKEAWAY Wisconsin: The Badgers added another win over an unranked team to their resume. But Wisconsin still needs help to make the playoff. Indiana: After trailing 24-17 heading into the fourth quarter, the Hoosiers added yet another chapter to a season filled with frustrating close calls . They're 0-3 against the top-five this season and 0-5 against the Top 25 this season. KEY NUMBERS Wisconsin: The Badgers finished with three takeaways. Hornibrook was 13 of 20 for 158 yards with two TDs and one interception. The Badgers held the ball for 39 minutes, 23 seconds and were 8 of 15 on third down. Indiana: Richard Lagow started in place of Ramsey and finished 20 of 34 for 226 yards with two TDs and two interceptions. The Hoosiers had 21 carries for 40 yards. Simmie Cobbs Jr., Whop Philyor and Luke Timian each had five catches. TOUGH CALL The momentum swung midway through the second quarter when a replay reversal gave the ball to Wisconsin at Indiana's 21-yard line. Initially, running back Morgan Ellison was ruled down by contact. But the call was reversed and the Indiana bench was subsequently called for unsportsmanlike conduct, moving the ball to the 11. Wisconsin scored its first points two plays later. POLL IMPLICATIONS Wisconsin: It's hard to imagine how the unbeaten Badgers move up in the eyes of voters -- or the selection committee -- unless or until the other top teams start losing. Indiana: The Hoosiers have the longest Top 25 drought of any power-conference school and that streak will remain intact. They haven't been ranked since September 1994. INJURY REPORT Wisconsin: Ingold started in place of Austin Ramesh, who did not make the trip. Inside linebacker Chris Orr (left leg) left in the first quarter and leading receiver Quintez Cephus (lower right leg) left in the third quarter. Neither returned. Indiana: Starting quarterback Peyton Ramsey sat out with a right knee injury. But the Hoosiers escaped another major injury when receiver Luke Timian got hit around the head as he was trying to field a second-quarter punt. Timian left briefly but later returned. UP NEXT Wisconsin: Returns home to face Iowa next Saturday in the first of back-to-back home games. Indiana: Travels to Illinois next Saturday, seeking its third straight win in the series. --- More AP college football: http://collegefootball.ap.org and http://www.twitter.com/AP-Top25

videos.headline: Wisconsin rallies past Indiana to remain undefeated
videos.description: Alec Ingold accounts for three touchdowns as Wisconsin improves to 9-0 with a 45-17 victory over Indiana.

videos.headline: Hornibrook picked off in end zone
videos.description: Wisconsin quarterback Alex Hornibrook throws a wobbly pass that is intercepted by Indiana's Tegray Scales in the end zone.

videos.headline: Williams gets wide open for Hoosier TD
videos.description: Richard Lagow finds Devonte Williams wide open on the left side for a 23-yard touchdown to give Indiana a 7-0 lead over Wisconsin.

videos.headline: Hornibrook finds Ingold wide open for Badger TD
videos.description: Alex Hornibrook uses the play-action to perfection as his fullback Alec Ingold gets open for an easy 18-yard touchdown for Wisconsin.

videos.headline: Cephus reaches over defender for Wisconsin TD
videos.description: Alex Hornibrook is hit as he throws  an 8-yard touchdown to Quintez Cephus who has to adjust and reach over his defender to make the catch.

videos.headline: Wisconsin's Cephus leaves game on crutches
videos.description: Badger wide receiver Quintez Cephus is forced to leave the game after being rolled over and injuring his lower leg.

videos.headline: Taylor bounces outside for big TD
videos.description: Wisconsin running back Jonathan Taylor breaks through the line and gets outside for a 32-yard touchdown padding the Badgers' lead.

videos.headline: Cobbs makes great grab for Hoosier TD
videos.description: Richard Laglow throws a perfect 17-yard touchdown to Simmie Cobbs Jr. in tight coverage for Indiana.

videos.headline: Ingold dives in for Badger TD
videos.description: Alec Ingold dives through the line for the 1-yard touchdown increasing Wisconsin's lead.

videos.headline: Taylor opens it up for Wisconsin with 45-yard run
videos.description: Badger running back Jonathan Taylor gets a huge gain for 45 yards on the first play from scrimmage.

videos.headline: Hornibrook connects with Taylor for 32 yards
videos.description: Alex Hornibrook floats a 32-yard pass to A.J. Taylor on third down to get Wisconsin inside Hoosier territory.

videos.headline: Ingold scores his third TD
videos.description: Wisconsin's Alec Ingold takes in another 1-yard touchdown  as the Badgers jump up 38-17 over Indiana.

videos.headline: Shaw cuts outside for Badger TD
videos.description: Bradrick Shaw pours it on for Wisconsin with another 1-yard touchdown putting the Badgers up 28.

videos.headline: Chryst: 'They've got a good defense'
videos.description: Wisconsin head coach Paul Chryst knew Indiana's defense could give them trouble early, and they did as his team enters halftime with a 14-10 lead.

videos.headline: Chryst: "Every week is tough in the Big Ten'
videos.description: Wisconsin head coach Paul Chryst likes how his team responded in the second half against Indiana. by taking advantage of forced turnovers.

videos.headline: Turnovers key for Wisconsin blowout
videos.description: Dave Pasch and Greg McElroy discuss how Wisconsin was able to take advantage of Indiana's mistakes in the Badgers' 10th straight win.
